title: Benefits of a USCIS Online Account | USCIS
content:
## Benefits of a USCIS Online Account
The USCIS online account allows you to file certain forms online and track your filings anytime from anywhere. The first step is to create an account .
Attorneys and representatives can create an account to manage your filings, but they cannot use your account.

## Benefits of Filing Online
Filing a form online is better than mailing a paper form because you can:
- Enter your information using a phone, tablet, or computer;
- Save your draft form and finish it at your own pace;
- Avoid common mistakes (for example, you cannot submit your form without signing);
- Pay your filing fee easily and securely;
- Avoid mailing delays; and
- Receive confirmation of form submission in your online account.
After you file a form, you can use your account to:
- Get your case history and status updates;
- Respond to Requests for Evidence (RFEs);
- Access most notices we send you; and
- Send us secure messages that we will answer.

## Using Your Account Without Filing Online
You can use your online account to link all of your case files, even if you filed by mail with a paper form, and view case status and history for each filing.
You can also change your address and reschedule biometrics appointments in your online account, regardless of whether you filed online or by paper.
Beyond filing forms and tracking your filings online, your online account lets you use other USCIS services such as FOIA FIRST and myE-Verify.

### FOIA FIRST: Request Records through the Freedom of Information Act or Privacy Act
You can use your USCIS online account to request your immigration record or A-File, another person's immigration record (with their approval), and non-A-file information such as USCIS policies, data, or communications.
Learn more about requesting records on the Request Records through the Freedom of Information Act or Privacy Act page.

### myE-Verify: Verify Your Employment Eligibility
myE-Verify is a free web-based service for everyone who works or is looking for a job in the United States. E-Verify is for employers; myE-Verify is for workers and job seekers.
Learn more about the benefits of using myE-Verify on the About myE-Verify page.
- How to Create a USCIS Online Account
